data.title: SingHealth opens off-site MRI centre at Dunearn Village
data.description:
It features three new machines, boosting annual capacity by up to 18,000 slots.

SingHealth has opened a new off-site magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) centre at Dunearn Village, with three machines boosting annual capacity by up to 18,000 slots.

The new MRI machines, operated in partnership with United Imaging Healthcare, are equipped with artificial intelligence (AI) functions that can reduce scan time by up to 25% for certain procedures.

Two of the units feature a bore of 75 centimetres (cm) in diametre, 5 cm larger than standard MRI bores on the market.

Each machine includes a ‘starlight’ interior lining designed to create a greater sense of space and increase patient comfort, particularly for those with claustrophobia.

The MRI rooms also have dynamic wall and ceiling systems that project nature-themed visuals and integrate with audio systems for music or other entertainment during scans.

The SingHealth MRI Centre has been operational since 2 January this year.

data.title: Tampines West's first mixed-use Pinery Residences to open for preview
data.description:
The development comprises 588 residential units across six 14-storey blocks. 

Pinery Residences, a mixed-use development by Hoi Hup Realty and Sunway MCL Limited, will open for preview from 14 to 24 March 2026, comprising 588 residential units across six 14-storey blocks, offering two- to five-bedroom layouts, with integrated resort-style facilities.

The development is the first mixed-use project in Tampines West, featuring direct underground access to Tampines West MRT Station on the Downtown Line, according to a press release.

The sales gallery is located along Tampines Central 7 and will be open from 11 a.m. to 8 p.m. on weekdays and 10 a.m. to 7 p.m. on weekends.

Appointed marketing agencies are ERA, Huttons, PropNex, and SRI. VIP booking is scheduled for 27 March, with public booking on 28 March 2026.

The project includes a 121,600-square-feet Pinery Mall with a supermarket, food court, food and beverage outlets, retail shops, an early childhood development centre, and a community plaza.

Residential and commercial spaces are separated for convenience, with the residential carpark accessible via Bedok Reservoir Road, whilst the commercial carpark is located in the basement, accessed from Tampines Street 94.

A dedicated residential drop-off and separate entrances provide exclusive access for residents, the press release said.

The development is situated within 1 kilometre of St. Hilda’s Primary School, whilst other nearby educational institutions include St. Hilda’s Secondary School, Junyuan Primary School, Temasek Polytechnic, and the Singapore University of Technology and Design.

Major retail and lifestyle hubs—including Our Tampines Hub, Tampines Mall, Tampines 1, Century Square, and Jewel Changi Airport—are also within easy reach.

Bedok Reservoir Park is a 10-minute walk away, the press release added.

The development is connected to the Pan Island Expressway, Tampines Expressway, and East Coast Parkway. It is part of the East Region masterplan, with nearby projects such as Punggol Digital District, Changi Aviation Park, and the Paya Lebar Air Base redevelopment.

The area will see further growth with Tampines Regional Centre and Changi Airport Terminal 5.

Resort-style amenities at Pinery Residences include the Vitality Sanctuary, featuring a 50-metre lap pool, hydrotherapy pool, spa showers, Tower Pavilion, yoga spaces, and garden pockets.

The Play Cove features a multipurpose court, playground, open play lawn, and BBQ pavilions. The Family Oasis includes The Pinery House clubhouse with function rooms, media and games lounges, a kids’ party room, outdoor family pool, jacuzzi cove, and garden spaces.

Residential units are fitted with Smeg and Samsung kitchen appliances, Hansgrohe and Duravit sanitary wares, and integrated smart home technology for door security and air-conditioning control, the press release said.

data.title: Cathay Pacific doubles fuel surcharges amidst jet fuel price surge
data.description:
The hike comes into effect on 18 March.

Cathay Pacific will increase fuel surcharges starting 18 March, following a sharp rise in jet fuel prices linked to recent developments in the Middle East.

The surcharge for short-haul flights will rise to $46.40 (HK$290) from $22.72 (HK$142), whilst that for medium-haul flights will increase to $86.56 (HK$541) from $42.24 (HK$264).

Long-haul flights will see the surcharge rise to $186.24 (HK$1,164) from $91.04 (HK$569).

The airline said that jet fuel prices have roughly doubled since March, prompting adjustments under its established surcharge mechanism.

The fuel surcharge applies to all tickets on a per-sector basis, regardless of fare type, and is included in fares quoted through the airline’s direct booking channels.

“The fuel surcharge is reviewed regularly and closely tracks the price of refined jet fuel,” Cathay Pacific said.

$1 = HK$6.12

data.title: SGX revamps Next 50 index with four additions
data.description:
Review adds UltraGreen.ai, GuocoLand, Haw Par, and Valuemax.

SGX Indices will add four companies to the iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index and its liquidity-weighted variant from the start of trading on 23 March.

The additions are Haw Par Corp, GuocoLand, UltraGreen.ai, and Valuemax Group, whilst Banyan Tree Holdings, SBS Transit, Geo Energy Resources, and COSCO SHIPPING International Singapore will be removed.

SGX said the March 2026 quarterly rebalancing review also marks the inclusion of UltraGreen.ai, which was listed on the SGX Mainboard in early December 2025.

The bourse operator said the indices use free-float, market capitalisation, and liquidity criteria to identify companies that are both sizeable and tradable. The benchmarks track the next cohort of 50 companies outside Singapore’s 30 largest listed firms by market capitalisation.

The next review of the iEdge Singapore Next 50 Indices is scheduled for June 2026.

data.title: Vin’s launches retail commercial paper at 4.5%
data.description:
Offering targets $1m to $2m.

Vin’s Holdings has launched a six-month Singapore dollar commercial paper offering for retail investors on the SDAX Exchange platform, marking its first such retail series in digital securities.

The instrument, named Vin’s 6-month SGD Commercial Paper Series 001 (Retail), carries a tenor of 181 days and an interest rate of 4.50% per annum.

The company said it expects to raise between $1m and $2m from the offer, although the final amount may be adjusted at the board’s discretion after taking retail demand into account.

Vin’s said proceeds from the fundraising will be used for the group’s general working capital.

The launch comes under the SDAX Multicurrency CP Programme and follows the company’s earlier announcements on 12 January and 23 January.

data.title: Seatrium completes Crescent Yard divestment as Mooreast closes acquisition
data.description:
Transactions closed on 11 March.

Seatrium has completed the divestment of its Crescent Yard in Singapore, whilst Mooreast Holdings has completed the acquisition of the property at 60 Shipyard Crescent, with both transactions closing on 11 March.

Seatrium said the Crescent Yard sale, together with other non-core asset divestments disclosed earlier, is expected to deliver more than $50m in annualised cost savings upon completion.

Mooreast said operations at the new facility are expected to start by the third quarter of 2026. It said the site will support the fabrication of high-value subsea foundations and serve as a logistics hub for equipment and block assembly.

The group said the acquisition will quadruple production capacity, lifting its ability to support floating offshore wind projects to between 1.5 GW and 2 GW a year from 0.5 GW currently. It also said the site’s 865-metre waterfront will allow its yard division to expand its customer base from specialist vessels to offshore rigs.

Mooreast had separately announced that the purchase of 60 Shipyard Crescent, including the existing plant, equipment, and machinery on the property, was completed by its wholly owned subsidiary, Mooreast Asia, on 11 March.

data.title: Daily Markets Briefing: STI down 0.17%; Top stock is Jardine Matheson
data.description:
Jardine Matheson is the top stock with a 2.21% increase.

The Straits Times Index (STI) closed at 4,855.330, down 0.17% on Thursday, 12 March, at 5:21 p.m.

Jardine Matheson was the most active stock, followed by ThaiBev (1.14%) and Wilmar International (1.08%).

DFI Retail Group and UOL were at the foot.

data.title: Singapore’s billionaire tally hits 55 on Forbes 2026 list
data.description:
Jason Chang is the country’s richest, ranking 204th globally.

Singapore now has 55 billionaires on the Forbes World’s Billionaires List 2026, up from 49 last year.

Jason Chang, chairman of ASE Technology Holding, is the country’s richest, ranking 204th globally with a net worth of $18.18b (US$14.2b).

Li Xiting, co-founder and chairman of Shenzhen Mindray Bio-Medical Electronics, ranks 284th globally with a net worth of $15.13b (US$11.82b).

Philip Ng and his brother Robert Ng, ranked 542nd and 557th globally with net worths of $9.22b (US$7.2b) and $9.09b (US$7.1b), respectively, control Far East Organization, Singapore’s largest private property developer and landlord.

Globally, the billionaire population reached a record 3,428, with their combined net worth hitting $25.73t (US$20.1t).

Elon Musk retained the top spot with a net worth of $1.07t (US$839b).

Other top billionaires include Larry Page with $328.96b (US$257b), Sergey Brin with $303.36b (US$237b), Jeff Bezos with $286.72b (US$224b), and Mark Zuckerberg with $284.16b (US$222b).

data.title: Peak Energy expands with 10‑MW rooftop solar buy in Singapore
data.description:
The company will supply fixed-price solar to corporations.

Peak Energy acquired a nearly 10-megawatt rooftop solar portfolio in Singapore from Maiora Renewable Energy, increasing the company’s capacity to provide fixed-price renewable energy to corporate buyers as Singapore works towards an upgraded 2030 solar target.

The acquisition allows Peak Energy to aggregate domestic rooftop capacity for industrial and commercial consumers, according to a press release.

This supply provides an alternative to electricity generation from imported natural gas, which exposes Singaporean companies to price volatility in global fuel markets.

Peak Energy is in discussions with commercial entities regarding long-term virtual power purchase agreements, which offer price stability through energy sourced from local grids.

Demand for domestic generation persists as cross-border low-carbon electricity imports remain in regulatory and infrastructure development stages.

Proposed changes to GHG Protocol Scope 2 Guidance also influence procurement strategies, with updates requiring renewable energy certificates to match the specific grid location of electricity consumption, favouring local production over international imports.

"Singapore's rooftop market still has headroom to meet the country's 2030 solar ambition," Gavin Adda, Chief Executive Officer of Peak Energy, said.

He added that, with Stonepeak's backing, the company can combine operating assets with developed ones to offer off-site Power Purchase Agreements.

Marzio Keiling, Managing Partner at Maiora Renewable Energy, said Maiora Renewable Energy will shift its development focus to Taiwan and the Philippines.

He added that the company sought a partner with the financial strength to manage the Singapore assets.

The deal establishes a source of renewable power for companies seeking to meet decarbonisation goals while managing energy costs.

Distributed generation remains a primary method for volume delivery in Singapore due to land constraints that prevent utility-scale projects, the press release said.

data.title: Quantinuum pushes quantum frontiers with ‘Helios’ launch
data.description:
Helios and new R&D hub to advance quantum computing in pharma and finance.

Quantinuum has established a new R&D and Operations Centre in Singapore, marking its formal expansion into the city, the company announced in a statement.

The centre will support collaboration with local researchers and industry partners across pharmaceuticals, materials science, finance, and other sectors, the company said.

The company also plans to deploy its Helios quantum computer in Singapore later this year, it said, with the deployment and new centre intended to deepen engagement with Singapore’s research and innovation ecosystem.

Quantinuum said the centre’s establishment is supported by the Singapore Economic Development Board and builds on its collaboration with the National Quantum Office through the National Quantum Computing Hub under the National Quantum Strategy.

Quantinuum said it is working with local startups, including Entropica through its Startup Partner Program, and has signed a memorandum of understanding with Squareroot8 to co-develop quantum communications applications.

The centre will bring Quantinuum staff together with local researchers and industry partners to co‑develop quantum computing solutions and support workforce and capability building in Singapore
